name: L-Tryptophan
effects: Supports serotonin and melatonin precursor pathways
contraindications: EMS history linked to contaminated products; purity matters

sources.title: Nutrients Involved in Serotonin and Melatonin Synthesis (NIH/PMC article)

description: Dietary amino acid metabolized toward serotonin and melatonin pathways; supplement benefits are limited and context-dependent.
summary: Essential amino acid precursor to serotonin/melatonin and vitamin B3; supplement efficacy for mood/sleep remains uncertain.
mechanism: Proposed: precursor loading may modestly increase serotonin/melatonin signaling, but human efficacy evidence is weak.
safetyNotes: Historical EMS outbreaks were linked to impurities.
interactions: Potential additive serotonergic effects with SSRIs/SNRIs/MAOIs.
dosage: No standard therapeutic dose established; older reports used ~1–6 g/day.
preparation: Capsules/powders; also present in protein foods.
regulatoryStatus: Dietary supplement ingredient in the US; FDA import restriction lifted in 2001.
mechanisms: Proposed: precursor loading may modestly increase serotonin/melatonin signaling, but human efficacy evidence is weak
